programme.title: PL STORIES EP26: THE GREATEST GAME EVER PLAYED
programme.sub-title: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? Join two ex-footballers, a seasoned commentator, and a top football journalist as they lock horns in a passionate roundtable discussion. The panel revisits iconic moments, dissecting tactics, and legendary performances. Expect banter, bold claims, and maybe even a few surprises, as they strive to settle the debate—or perhaps keep it alive. Whether reliving “Fergie Time” magic, Leicester’s miracle season, or Aguero’s legendary finish, this episode celebrates the unfiltered drama that only Premier League history can offer.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP32: JACK TAYLOR
programme.sub-title: To celebrate Non-League Day, this week The Big Interview travels to Portman Road to speak with Ipswich Town's, Jack Taylor. Having scored in England's top 5 leagues, Taylor talks through his career from Barnet to the Premier League. The 26-year-old also shares his views on Ipswich's season to date, the impact Kieran McKenna's had on his game as well as his hopes for the future.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.sub-title: Jermain Defoe scored 162 Premier League goals, and it was at Tottenham where he was at his prolific best. The striker recalls his two spells with the North London club, including the memorable occasion where he hit 5 in one match.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP06: TIM SHERWOOD: MY GREATEST SEASON
programme.sub-title: Blackburn Rovers' Premier League title win in 1994/95 was a historic achievement. Under the guidance of Kenny Dalglish, and fuelled by the prolific partnership of Alan Shearer and Chris Sutton, the small-town club dethroned Manchester United and reached the pinnacle of English football. Captain Tim Sherwood recalls the key moments.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: DIDIER DROGBA HALL OF FAME SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: He scored 104 Premier League goals, making 55 assists in 254 English top flight matches for Chelsea. programme.sub-title: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. We dive deep into his rise as one of Africa's finest football talents, and how the near-death tragedy profoundly impacted his life, shaping his resilience and outlook beyond the game.

programme.desc: After a meteoric rise through Cardiff’s youth system, Aaron Ramsey’s dream of playing for one of the world’s biggest clubs was realised. But just as his career was reaching new heights, a devastating double leg break threatened to bring it all crashing down. The Wales captain takes us through his footballing journey to date.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: CHAMPIONS SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: Season 2023 - 2024 has arguably been the greatest since the birth of the Premier League in 1992. Three elite teams racing to the finish line. Each of them believing this season will end in glory. The script of the story is captivating. Manchester City going for an historic fourth consecutive Premier League title. Arsenal looking for their first title in twenty years following their late season collapse in 2023. Liverpool looking to give their manager Jurgen Klopp the perfect send off as he says good-bye to Anfield after nine years. With the help of leading celebrity fans, the managers and senior players each twist and turn of the season is highlighted. The narrative of the season is like no other. The Champions Story 2023 - 2024 tells a very special story.
programme.desc: Season 2023 - 2024 has arguably been the greatest since the birth of the Premier League in 1992. Three elite teams racing to the finish line. programme.title: PL REWIND EP06: TIM SHERWOOD: MY GREATEST SEASON
programme.sub-title: Blackburn Rovers' Premier League title win in 1994/95 was a historic achievement. Under the guidance of Kenny Dalglish, and fuelled by the prolific partnership of Alan Shearer and Chris Sutton, the small-town club dethroned Manchester United and reached the pinnacle of English football. Captain Tim Sherwood recalls the key moments.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP10: MICHAEL OWEN: MY BREAKTHROUGH YEARS
programme.sub-title: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. Owen recalls those campaigns for the Reds, reliving the key moments that helped him develop into one of the greatest players of his generation.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP24: (Subbed) SAMI HYYPIA
programme.sub-title: From his early days in Finland to lifting the Champions League trophy in 2005, we chart Sami Hyypiä’s journey to the top of English football. We join him for a snowmobiling adventure across the icy wilderness of Finland and though interviews and personal moments, showcase not only his leadership and skill on the pitch but also his deep connection to his roots.
programme.desc: From his early days in Finland to lifting the Champions League trophy in 2005, we chart Sami Hyypiä’s journey to the top of English football. We join him for a snowmobiling adventure across the icy wilderness of Finland and though interviews and personal moments, showcase not only his leadership and skill on the pitch but also his deep connection to his roots.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP25: (Subbed) HERNAN CRESPO
programme.sub-title: We delve into the remarkable journey of Argentine football legend Hernán Crespo. From his early days as a striker with clubs like Parma, Lazio, and Chelsea, to his evolution into a title winning coach, Crespo's career has been filled with highs and challenges. We follow his transition from player to manager and join him at Al Ain, where we get a look into how he navigates the pressures and ambitions of managing one of the UAE's top football clubs.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP15: TIM CAHILL
programme.sub-title: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP32: JACK TAYLOR
programme.sub-title: To celebrate Non-League Day, this week The Big Interview travels to Portman Road to speak with Ipswich Town's, Jack Taylor. Having scored in England's top 5 leagues, Taylor talks through his career from Barnet to the Premier League. The 26-year-old also shares his views on Ipswich's season to date, the impact Kieran McKenna's had on his game as well as his hopes for the future.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: KICKS AND CULTURE STATESIDE
programme.sub-title: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. The show is led by Aaron Dolores, football fan and founder of Black Arrow, a creative movement on a quest to promote the intersection of football and black culture, not just in the US but worldwide. He takes us on a journey where we meet varying characters from the Mayor of Birmingham Alabama, Randall Woodfin, former players Tim Howard and Jozy Altidore, to current player Chris Richards as well as authentic Black American fans immersed in Premier League culture.

Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
programme.desc: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP29: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP43: (Subbed) EDUARDO DA SILVA
programme.sub-title: Eduardo da Silva will forever be synonymous for suffering one of the worst injuries in Premier League history. We travelled to Brazil, Croatia and caught up with the former Arsenal hero here in England to find out more about the man who gave Gunners fans a fleeting glimpse of greatness before being so cruelly deprived.
programme.desc: Eduardo da Silva will forever be synonymous for suffering one of the worst injuries in Premier League history. We travelled to Brazil, Croatia and caught up with the former Arsenal hero here in England to find out more about the man who gave Gunners fans a fleeting glimpse of greatness before being so cruelly deprived.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: (Subbed) ARSENE WENGER AND HIS MANAGERIAL JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.
programme.desc: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. programme.title: PL MOMENTS EP08: EMMANUEL ADEBAYOR
programme.sub-title: Join Togo and African football icon Emmanuel Adebayor as he relives his top 10 Premier League moments. From iconic goals to unforgettable rivalries, this inspiring and entertaining show is packed with candid insights, untold stories, and the passion that defined his career.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP20: (Subbed) EMMANUEL ADEBAYOR
programme.sub-title: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. We dive deep into his rise as one of Africa's finest football talents, and how the near-death tragedy profoundly impacted his life, shaping his resilience and outlook beyond the game.
programme.desc: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. programme.title: PL STORIES EP08: (Subbed) PREMIER LEAGUE MAVERICKS

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP33: LUIS GARCIA
programme.sub-title: Arriving on Merseyside in 2004 as part of a freshly recruited Spanish contingent to help build a new-look Liverpool side, Luis Garcia soon became a cult hero at Anfield. But it wasn’t always plain sailing for the Catalonian. In this week’s Big Interview, Garcia shares the difficulties he faced adjusting to the Premier League, how Rafa Benitez managed to hone his talents and his thoughts on that famous “ghost” goal against Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-final.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP33: LUIS GARCIA
programme.sub-title: Arriving on Merseyside in 2004 as part of a freshly recruited Spanish contingent to help build a new-look Liverpool side, Luis Garcia soon became a cult hero at Anfield. But it wasn’t always plain sailing for the Catalonian. In this week’s Big Interview, Garcia shares the difficulties he faced adjusting to the Premier League, how Rafa Benitez managed to hone his talents and his thoughts on that famous “ghost” goal against Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-final.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.
